title: 𒉆𒈗
part: Antediluvian prologue · the five cities and the Flood (lines 1–41)
draftNote: Draft in progress — the cuneiform and its readings are provisional, checked sign-by-sign against Langdon's 1923 edition of the prism, so expect changes as the text is extended.
source: Weld-Blundell Prism (WB 444), after S. Langdon, Oxford Editions of Cuneiform Texts II (Oxford, 1923), Col. I. Cuneiform in normalized Unicode (Neo-Assyrian forms) though the prism is Old Babylonian. Academic transliteration in modern subscript notation over Langdon's 1923 readings; pronunciation conventional/reconstructed. Fresh translations.
about: The world's oldest king-list, copied c. 1800 BCE: the five cities that held kingship before the Flood, and the Flood itself. Every layer — sign readings, values, word-divisions, pronunciation, even the giant numbers — is a scholarly convention, some frank guesses; the notes flag genuine uncertainty rather than hide it. One royal name Langdon read from a damaged sign (Ubara-Tutu, at Shuruppak) is shown 'glyphs pending' while its reading stands.

sentences.i: When kingship came down from heaven,
sentences.n: The famous opening. nam- abstract prefix on lugal 'king'; -ta ablative 'from'; an 'heaven'. e₃ 'go up/down' + -a + -ba: 'when it had descended.' You'll meet nam-, -ta, -a constantly. The sign read e₃ (𒂊) is one conventional spelling of this verb.
-la is the genitive/locative: Eridu holds the kingship. KI after Eridu is a determinative — an unpronounced place-marker; tap it.

The first king. Langdon read A-lu-lim but flagged its final sign (his 'REC 229'); it is the IGI sign carrying the value lim — a non-obvious reading, now resolved. Alulim is listed as a mortal, with no divine determinative.

morph.gloss: Alulim — first king of Eridu; final sign is IGI read lim (Langdon's uncertain 'REC 229')

sentences.n: The reign formula, repeated for every king: mu ('years') + number + verb. Langdon reads ni-ag; modern editions read ì-aĝ₂ 'measured out (rule)'. Numbers are sexagesimal: 28,800 = 8 × 3,600.

morph.gloss: ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: Langdon writes A-la(l)-gar, the '(l)' marking a sign he read tentatively.

morph.gloss: Alalgar — second king of Eridu (Langdon A-la(l)-gar)
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: Eridu's total: two kings. '2' is two vertical wedges (𒁹𒁹).
ib-ag (íb-aĝ₂) is the plural 'they ruled'. 64,800 = 28,800 + 36,000.
The refrain that ends every dynasty: the city falls (ba-šub) and kingship moves on. šub is written with the RU sign — a sign with more than one value, normal in cuneiform.

morph.gloss: 'fell, was abandoned' (šub written with the RU sign)

sentences.n: -šú (the EŠ₂ sign) is the terminative 'to / towards' — kingship moves on. Bad-tibira: bàd₃ 'fortress' is written EZEN×BAD + DUB; tibira 'metalworker' is the NAGAR sign. Langdon footnotes tibira, linking it to the Tubal-Cain of Genesis.

morph.gloss: Bad-tibira — second city: bàd₃ 'fortress' (EZEN×BAD, with DUB) + tibira 'metalworker' (NAGAR) + place-det ki

sentences.n: ba-ĝen 'went, passed' — the verb ĝen 'to go' is written with the DU sign (𒁺), which also gives gub, du, rá.

morph.gloss: ba-ĝen — 'went, passed' (ĝen written with the DU sign)

sentences.n: Enmenluanna, first king of Bad-tibira — an 'En-men-…-anna' throne-name (en 'lord', an 'heaven') shared by several pre-flood kings.

morph.gloss: Bad-tibira — second city: bàd₃ 'fortress' (EZEN×BAD, with DUB) + tibira 'metalworker' (NAGAR) + place-det ki
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: A throne-name in the 'En-men-…-anna' pattern shared by several pre-flood kings (en 'lord', an 'heaven').

morph.gloss: ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: {d} is the divine determinative (diĝir) before the name. sib 'shepherd' is written PA (staff, 𒉺) + LU (sheep, 𒇻) — 'staff-over-sheep'. This Dumuzi, listed as a mortal king here, becomes the dying shepherd-god of later myth.

morph.gloss: divine determinative (diĝir) — marks the following name as a god
Dumuzi 'the shepherd' — a pre-flood king (later the dying shepherd-god)
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: Bad-tibira's total: three kings.
108,000 = three šaru (3 × 36,000), one sign each.
Bad-tibira falls; kingship passes to Larak (next). ba-šub-bi-en is the fall-refrain that closes every dynasty.

morph.gloss: Bad-tibira — second city: bàd₃ 'fortress' (EZEN×BAD, with DUB) + tibira 'metalworker' (NAGAR) + place-det ki
'fell, was abandoned' (šub written with the RU sign)

sentences.i: its kingship was established at Larak;
sentences.n: Larak (la-ra-ak), the fourth pre-flood city. ba-gub 'was set up' — again the DU sign, here read gub.

morph.gloss: ba-gub — 'was set up, established' (gub written with the DU sign)

sentences.n: sib 'shepherd' again (PA·LU) inside the royal name.

morph.gloss: Ensipazianna — the one king of Larak
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: Larak had a single king.
Larak falls; kingship will pass to Sippar (next).

morph.gloss: 'fell, was abandoned' (šub written with the RU sign)

sentences.n: Sippar (Sumerian Zimbir) is written UD.KIB.NUN — the KIB sign is 'gish-crossing-gish' (𒄒). The city of the sun-god Utu.

morph.gloss: Sippar (Sumerian Zimbir) — the fifth pre-flood city; written UD.KIB.NUN + ki
ba-ĝen — 'went, passed' (ĝen written with the DU sign)

sentences.n: dúr is the DUR₂ sign. This king becomes Enmeduranki, the mythic first diviner, in later tradition.

morph.gloss: Sippar (Sumerian Zimbir) — the fifth pre-flood city; written UD.KIB.NUN + ki
Enmenduranna — the one king of Sippar (the Enmeduranki of later divination lore)

sentences.n: lugal-am₃ 'was king' — the enclitic copula -am₃, written a-an. 21,000 = five šar₂ (18,000) + five geš'u (3,000).

morph.gloss: enclitic copula 'is / was' — written am₃ = a-an
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg
Sippar (Sumerian Zimbir) — the fifth pre-flood city; written UD.KIB.NUN + ki
'fell, was abandoned' (šub written with the RU sign)

sentences.i: its kingship was established at Shuruppak;
sentences.n: Shuruppak (SU.KUR.RU) — the last city before the Flood, and the home of Ziusudra, the Sumerian Noah. This is the city of the Instructions of Shuruppak.

morph.gloss: Shuruppak — the last pre-flood city, home of Ziusudra (the Flood hero); written SU.KUR.RU + ki
ba-gub — 'was set up, established' (gub written with the DU sign)

sentences.n: Ubara-Tutu, the last pre-flood king and father of Ziusudra (the Flood hero). Langdon reads the first sign as ubur, the 'breast' sign, which has no settled single Unicode form — so this line is held back while the reading stands. (du-du and Shuruppak itself do resolve.)

morph.gloss: enclitic copula 'is / was' — written am₃ = a-an
ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg

sentences.n: The summary of the whole pre-flood age. eri-me-eš 'they are cities' — eri 'city' + the plural copula -me-eš.
The grand total. Langdon writes it šar-gal + 7 šar₂: šar-gal is the 'great šar₂' (216,000 = 60³), the next power up. 216,000 + 25,200 = 241,200, which is exactly the eight reigns added together.
a-ma-ru, the Deluge — 'the great water'. The single line on which the whole antediluvian age ends. Everything before it is myth-time; after it, kingship begins again.

morph.gloss: the Flood, the Deluge (a-ma-ru, lit. 'great water')

sentences.i: After the Flood had swept over,
sentences.n: egir 'after'. The narrative turns to resume: kingship will descend from heaven a second time (to Kish).

morph.gloss: the Flood, the Deluge (a-ma-ru, lit. 'great water')

sentences.i: (the kingship that had come down from heaven) —
sentences.n: Here the scribe absent-mindedly re-copied the opening line — Langdon marks it '(Sic!)'. A 3,800-year-old copying slip, preserved. The sentence properly belongs only at the very start; its reappearance here is an error, not part of the text.

glossary.a₂-lu-lim: Alulim — first king of Eridu; final sign is IGI read lim (Langdon's uncertain 'REC 229')
glossary.šar₂: šar₂ — 3,600 (60 × 60); the working unit for the reign-lengths
glossary.ni-ag: ì-aĝ₂ (Langdon 'ni-ag') — 'he ruled', lit. 'measured out'; reign verb, 3sg
glossary.a-la-gar: Alalgar — second king of Eridu (Langdon A-la(l)-gar)
glossary.šar'u: šar'u — 36,000 (ten šar₂, folded into one sign)
glossary.ba-šub: 'fell, was abandoned' (šub written with the RU sign)
glossary.bàd₃-tibira: Bad-tibira — second city: bàd₃ 'fortress' (EZEN×BAD, with DUB) + tibira 'metalworker' (NAGAR) + place-det ki
glossary.ba-gín: ba-ĝen — 'went, passed' (ĝen written with the DU sign)
glossary.{d}: divine determinative (diĝir) — marks the following name as a god
glossary.dumu-zi-sib: Dumuzi 'the shepherd' — a pre-flood king (later the dying shepherd-god)
glossary.ba-gub: ba-gub — 'was set up, established' (gub written with the DU sign)
glossary.en-sib-zi-an-na: Ensipazianna — the one king of Larak
glossary.zimbir: Sippar (Sumerian Zimbir) — the fifth pre-flood city; written UD.KIB.NUN + ki
glossary.en-me-en-dúr-an-na: Enmenduranna — the one king of Sippar (the Enmeduranki of later divination lore)
glossary.-am₃: enclitic copula 'is / was' — written am₃ = a-an
glossary.šuruppak: Shuruppak — the last pre-flood city, home of Ziusudra (the Flood hero); written SU.KUR.RU + ki
glossary.a-ma-ru: the Flood, the Deluge (a-ma-ru, lit. 'great water')
